subject: Lane_01 CTO review request for AIER Code token/context optimization protocol
body: Please review packet AMD_LANE03_AIER_CODE_TOKEN_CONTEXT_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26 and target file docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/CANON/aier_code_token_and_context_optimization_protocol_v_1_draft.md. Requested review points: whether the 5-layer context loading model is safe; whether BOOT_MINIMUM + CURRENT_STATE + TASK_CONTEXT + TARGET_FILES is sufficient; whether the document should remain in CANON or later move/reference into SHARED/boot or SHARED/os_operations; whether it conflicts with Memory, Communication, OS Operations, or Roadmap Spec; whether estimates such as +5-15% overhead and -50-80% drift should remain or be softened; and whether implementation should split into Boot Minimum, Task Context, and Document Stack Index tasks. Boundary: review evidence only, not approval; NTS final approval is required; Lane_02 is deferred for this phase. Requested verdicts: ENDORSE / AMEND / OBJECT / SPLIT / DEFER.
requested_action: Provide Lane_01 CTO review evidence for the AIER Code token/context optimization protocol packet, including a verdict of ENDORSE, AMEND, OBJECT, SPLIT, or DEFER.
acceptance_criteria: Lane_01 reviews the safety of the five-layer context loading model.
Lane_01 reviews whether BOOT_MINIMUM + CURRENT_STATE + TASK_CONTEXT + TARGET_FILES is sufficient.
Lane_01 recommends whether the document should remain in CANON or later move/reference into SHARED/boot or SHARED/os_operations.
Lane_01 checks for conflicts with Memory, Communication, OS Operations, and Roadmap Spec.
Lane_01 recommends whether estimates should remain or be softened.
Lane_01 recommends whether implementation should split into separate Boot Minimum, Task Context, and Document Stack Index tasks.
Lane_01 returns ENDORSE, AMEND, OBJECT, SPLIT, or DEFER.
